examples: Seems like only last week she was still just a bairn.
Who has taught you those fine words, my bairn?' I inquired. 'The curate?'
I can't believe she's going off to university. Seems like only last week she was still just a bairn.
Scots words like bairn (child), midden (dump), muckle (large) and even kilt (from the verb kjalta, meaning "to fold") are derived from Old Norse.
